UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a student's profile within the SERS application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

contactPhoneNumber.description: The student's primary contact phone number.

emergencyContactPhoneNumber.description: Phone number of the emergency contact.

medicalInformation.description: Any relevant medical information the student wishes to share.

IncidentReport.title: IncidentReport
IncidentReport.description: Represents a reported incident within the SERS application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the IncidentReport entity.

incidentType.description: Type of incident reported (Fire, Medical, GBV, Bullying, Crime).

detailedDescription.description: A detailed description of the incident.

reportDateTime.description: Date and time the incident was reported.

mediaUrls.description: List of URLs pointing to media files uploaded with the report.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Path-based ownership ensures only the authenticated user can access their own profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores incident reports submitted by users. Path-based ownership ensures only the authenticated user can access their own reports.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique identifier of the incident report.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, following the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, and QAPs. 

User profiles are stored in a private collection `/users/{userId}/profile` to ensure only the authenticated user can access their own profile data. Incident reports are stored as subcollections under each user's document (`/users/{userId}/incidentReports/{reportId}`), clearly establishing ownership. This design prioritizes path-based ownership, enhancing security rule simplicity and efficiency. There is no denormalization required, since Authorization Independence is achieved with path-based ownership.

Structural segregation is achieved by separating user profiles and incident reports into distinct collections and subcollections, respectively. This allows for homogeneous security postures: `/users/{userId}/profile` for private user data and `/users/{userId}/incidentReports/{reportId}` for user-owned incident reports.

The structure is designed to support secure `list` operations (QAPs) by leveraging path-based ownership. Security rules can easily filter based on `request.auth.uid` to ensure users can only access their own incident reports. Global roles or custom claims are not used, adhering to DBAC principles.
